Deep Learning Guide: Deep learning is a subset of machine learning that uses neural networks with many layers to model complex patterns in data. Convolutional neural networks (CNNs) excel at image recognition tasks, while recurrent networks (RNNs) and transformers handle sequential data like text and audio. Training deep models requires large datasets, substantial compute (typically GPUs), and careful hyperparameter tuning. Frameworks like PyTorch and TensorFlow provide high-level APIs that abstract away much of the low-level implementation complexity.
